title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
summary: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.

title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
summary: Saudi Arabia has for years sought a deal to obtain American nuclear technology. The agreement, signed on Wednesday, could deepen U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

title: Mamdani Knew He Couldn’t Arrest Netanyahu. But He Could Denounce Him.
summary: In a video message, the mayor of New York City said he could not legally arrest Israel’s prime minister but reinforced his position as a voice of the international left.

title: Trump Administration Pressures Some Immigrants to Self-Deport With Fines Up to $1.8 Million
summary: Tens of thousands of immigrants have received letters to pay up or leave, as the government enlists debt collectors and seizes tax refunds.

title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
summary: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

title: South Carolina and Nevada Fight to Kick Off Democrats’ 2028 Presidential Calendar
summary: The calendar order could be hugely important to the 2028 presidential primary race. Democratic insiders will try to sketch out a schedule this week, and rival states are sniping at one another.

title: Massachusetts House Passes Bill That Would Loosen Late-Term Abortion Rules
summary: The bill would leave it to physicians to decide if an abortion is necessary after 24 weeks of pregnancy. Current law allows such abortions only under certain circumstances.

title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.

title: Former Head of All-Girls School Indicted, Accused of Ignoring Abuse Reports
summary: Jean Norris, who ran Miss Hall’s School in Massachusetts, was charged with reckless endangerment of a child. A former teacher under her tenure faces three rape charges.

title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

title: How to Eat a 2,000-Pound Fish? These Orcas Choose to Pulverize It.
summary: Marine biologists in Mexico captured video of a strange maneuver that killer whales used to shred their meal.

title: Haiti Needs Stability, Not Mass Deportations
summary: Deporting Haitian migrants will further destabilize a country in crisis.

title: Gen Z-ers Are Ditching Sex. They Might Be Onto Something.
Late Night Rags on Trump and His Cabinet Joining TikTok
summary: “Right now TikTok is, like, ‘Oh, no, are we the new Facebook?’” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show.”

title: Deep in This Lake, a Contagious Cancer Is Spreading
summary: Transmissible tumors have been reported in a handful of animal species, but never before among fish. Is it possible in humans?
